creator: tom@tomqnx.com
text:
Even when running '-q' routed kills the default route when the line
hangs up.  It also makes it impossible to reinstall the default route
manually (network unconnected).  This makes it impossible to run ijppp.
Note that if routed is then killed (TERM) the default still cannot be
added manually.
The old routed would kill the default route once, then if manually
added it would leave it alone, allowing ijppp to run long term.

Fix: 

I suggest a command-line options that will cause routed to NEVER
remove the default for any reason.
How-To-Repeat: Run the new routed with ijppp -auto.  It won't last long.
